economic_notes: First Nation undertaking diesel generating station upgrade project with Hydro One Remote Communities Inc. for new 1500 kW generator, estimated cost $12.20 million, completion 2025. New water treatment plant under construction. CMHC rapid housing project underway. Portfolio investment of $255,000 in Happy Time Tours and Travel (15% ownership). Guarantee provided to BMO for Happy Time Tours credit facility of $1,039,617.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) incomplete tangible capital asset inventory — First Nation has not gathered information to recognize a complete inventory under PS 3150; (2) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not adopted as required from April 1, 2022. Neskantaga Economic Development Corporation is fully consolidated. Capital Projects segment shows large surplus ($9.6M) due to deferred revenue recognition timing on major infrastructure projects (water treatment plant, police detachment, CMHC rapid housing). Large increase in deferred revenue from $18.2M to $30.5M driven by ISC funding received but not yet recognized. Public Safety Canada NAPS building deferred revenue of $5.07M outstanding. Province identified as Ontario based on auditor location (Thunder Bay) and Ontario ministry references.
